---
title: "Confirmation Bias"
categories: [heuristics, overconfidence]
---

This bias refers to registering or recalling information that supports one’s pre-existing views while ignoring contradictory evidence.

::: {.callout-note icon=false collapse="false"}
## Example

#### Media coverage
A reader notices when the newspaper mentions an immigrant being arrested for theft (confirms a stereotype) but turns the page when it is a local  arrested for the same crime.

!["Arghhh, those immigrants again."](../images/confirmation-bias.jpg){width="600px" fig-align="center"}

::: {.also-relates}
**Also relates to:** [Self-Attribution Bias](self-attribution-bias.qmd) · [Availability Heuristic](availability-heuristic.qmd) · [Communal Reinforcement](communal-reinforcement.qmd) · [Hindsight Bias](hindsight-bias.qmd) · [Causality and Attribution](causality-and-attribution.qmd) · [Illusion of Validity](illusion-of-validity.qmd)
:::

:::
